headlines.title: UK inflation rate rises to 3.3% in March after Iran war pushes up fuel prices
headlines.description: The figures provide the first official look at the impact of the Iran war on the cost of living in the UK.

headlines.title: PM sends 'chill' through civil service, its union boss says
headlines.description: The 'chill' follows the sacking of lead civil servant at the Foreign Office Sir Olly Robbins by the prime minister.

headlines.title: I was left with an £8,000 vet bill when my insurer cancelled my pet policy
headlines.description: Thousands of people have got in touch with BBC Your Voice over concerns about rising pet insurance costs and poor cover.

headlines.title: How Leicester went from Premier League champions to League One in a decade
headlines.description: Ten years ago, Leicester were days away from winning the Premier League. Next season, they will play in League One. How?

headlines.title: Ideal conditions to see peak of Lyrid meteor shower in UK
headlines.description: The Lyrid meteor shower is the oldest recorded and Wednesday brings near perfect weather conditions to see them as they peak. Simon King explains.

headlines.title: Bird flu vaccine trial against potential pandemic strain begins
headlines.description: The jab targets the H5N1 flu strain which has caused devastating infections in bird populations worldwide, but has yet to spread between humans.

headlines.title: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
headlines.description: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

headlines.title: Meta to track workers' clicks and keystrokes to train AI
headlines.description: The firm will take data from the way employees work for its artificial intelligence models.

headlines.title: Tourist finds rare chunk of oldest sea crocodile
headlines.description: The Thalattosuchian's jawbone was a chance find on a guided fossil walk run by a museum.

headlines.title: Foo Fighters interview: 'We're a different band without Taylor Hawkins'
headlines.description: The band talk about life without their drummer, their "angry" new album, and their backstage Lego habit.

headlines.title: 400 job applications but only three interviews - the jobseeker feeling 'ghosted' by employers
headlines.description: The term ghosting is common in the dating world - but job applicants are increasingly reporting it.

headlines.title: 50 days until World Cup - pick your favourite kits
headlines.description: BBC Sport has highlighted some of the most interesting kits which will be seen at this summer's World Cup. Pick your favourites.

headlines.title: UK gaming icon Peter Molyneux on AI, his final creation and a changing industry
headlines.description: The creator of iconic series such as Fable says Masters of Albion will be the last game he makes.

headlines.title: A Kashmir tourist hotspot became a bloodbath. A year on, the pain remains unbearable
headlines.description: A year after militants killed 26 people in Indian-administered Kashmir, families are trying to cope with their loss.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Starmer on the ropes' and 'Sobbin' Robbins spills the beans'
headlines.description: The papers are dominated by the fallout for the prime minister from the failed vetting of Lord Mandelson for his role as British ambassador to the US.

headlines.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
headlines.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

headlines.title: World's biggest condom maker set to raise prices due to Iran war
headlines.description: Malaysia-based Karex produces more than five billion condoms a year and supplies global brands like Durex and Trojan.

headlines.title: Beverley Callard forced to leave I'm A Celebrity: 'I didn't know I had cancer'
headlines.description: The soap star revealed her diagnosis in February, several months after the reality show was filmed.

headlines.title: Man, 77, slapped traffic warden and threatened to 'lump' her
headlines.description: Stephen Doughty assaulted Wendy Williams after struggling to use a pay-and-display machine.

headlines.title: Dwayne Johnson wrestling film to be made into stage musical
headlines.description: Fighting With My Family, released in 2019, starred Florence Pugh as a young wrestler making her name.

headlines.title: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots snapping pictures, report finds
headlines.description: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

headlines.title: O'Sullivan starts well in bid for eighth World Championship
headlines.description: Ronnie O'Sullivan begins his quest for a record-breaking eighth World Championship title with a dominant opening session against He Guoqiang.

headlines.title: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
headlines.description: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Global Story: How Pakistan became the peacemaker
headlines.description: Talks are expected between the US and Iran in Islamabad.

headlines.title: The Prime Minister v Olly Robbins
headlines.description: Sacked Foreign Office chief describes 'dismissive' attitude to Mandelson vetting.

headlines.title: 'Indefensible' and 'unacceptable' - furious Rosenior questions Chelsea's desire
headlines.description: Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior strongly criticises his players after they suffer a fifth loss in a row without scoring for first time since 1912.

headlines.title: How Leicester went from Premier League champions to League One in a decade
headlines.description: Ten years ago, Leicester were days away from winning the Premier League. Next season, they will play in League One. How?

headlines.title: 50 days until World Cup - pick your favourite kits
headlines.description: BBC Sport has highlighted some of the most interesting kits which will be seen at this summer's World Cup. Pick your favourites.

headlines.title: Chelsea to play all WSL games at Stamford Bridge
headlines.description: Chelsea say switching all their women's games to Stamford Bridge reaffirms an "ambition and intent" to become "the biggest women's sports club in the world".

headlines.title: Brighton beat Chelsea to heap more pressure on Rosenior
headlines.description: Brighton heap more pressure on Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior as they ease to a comfortable 3-0 win thanks to goals from Ferdi Kadioglu, Jack Hinshelwood and Danny Welbeck.

headlines.title: James shines for Lakers while Wembanyama concussed
headlines.description: LeBron James leads the LA Lakers into a 2-0 series lead over the Houston Rockets, while Victor Wembanyama faces a race to be fit for the San Antonio Spurs' next game against the Portland Trail Blazers.
